mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0% signals labor market deterioration that immediately triggers equity sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 5.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and deceleration in retail sales indicates demand destruction from price shocks, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 5% and RSAFS growth turns negative over consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects expanding corporate output capacity, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to correlate with positive 20-day returns during recovery periods, with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and corporate profit expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months where PAYEMS increases by more than 100000.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 2.0% triggers immediate portfolio rebalancing away from equities toward fixed income, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of observations when FEDFUNDS crosses the 2.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and real GDP growth confirm broad-based economic expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases when both series show quarter-over-quarter growth.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals supply chain bottlenecks and profit margin compression, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Negative returns do not materialize when INDPRO falls for two consecutive months during late-cycle phases, with out-of-sample accuracy below 45%.
